event: 2024-06-12 crvUSD spot dislocation (FALSE-POSITIVE DISCIPLINE CONTROL) — during the 2024-06-12 CRV liquidation cascade, crvUSD SPOT prices in thin individual Curve pools briefly wicked UPWARD toward ~$1.10 (transient) and the PegKeeper V2 deviation check (±0.0005 band) reverted for ~45 min, BUT the manipulation-resistant crvUSD EMA AGGREGATOR — the price() that Curve LlamaLend markets actually denominate against and a lending monitor ingests — HELD at $0.99842760 (REAL on-chain MEASURED @ block 20075607). The ingested price never left the peg band; only thin spot pools wicked. The monitor MUST stay quiet (no DEPEG). Complements the USDe CEX-wick control (off-chain wick) with an on-chain spot-vs-EMA-oracle case. See EVENT.md.
source: LlamaRisk crvUSD Upward Depeg incident report 2024-06-12 (https://llamarisk.com/research/crvusd-incident-report-20240612): aggregator EMA held peg vs thin pool spot; PegKeeper ±0.0005 deviation-check revert ~45 min/~525 blocks; LlamaLend denominates against the aggregator; full liquidation, no bad debt. See EVENT.md.

crvUSD-llamalend-collateral.source: onchain:crvUSD-aggregator.price() (0xa035b1fe) @ethereum:20075607 = 998427599842524685/1e18 = $0.99842760 (REAL on-chain MEASURED, this re-capture; deviation -0.157% vs $1.00, in-band); thin spot pools wicked to ~$1.10 transiently (documented, NOT the ingested price)

cases.note: ON-CHAIN SPOT-vs-EMA-ORACLE FP CONTROL. During the 2024-06-12 CRV liquidation cascade, liquidators bought crvUSD to repay a large CRV-collateralized crvUSD debt, and crvUSD SPOT in thin individual Curve pools transiently wicked UPWARD toward ~$1.10. The PegKeeper V2 deviation check (±0.0005 band) reverted, so PegKeepers could not supply crvUSD for ~45 min (~525 blocks) — a property of thin pool spot, not of the ingested oracle. The price a lending monitor consumes is the manipulation-resistant crvUSD EMA AGGREGATOR (0x18672b1b…4E62), which all Curve LlamaLend markets denominate against — NOT raw pool spot. That aggregator price() = $0.99842760 @ block 20075607 (REAL on-chain MEASURED this re-capture: eth_call selector 0xa035b1fe -> raw 998427599842524685/1e18 = $0.99842759842524685; previously modelled as the rounded 0.9984), deviation -0.157% vs $1.00, inside the stable peg band. So the ingested mark never left peg; only thin spot pools wicked, transiently and upward, with no bad debt accrued. by_design_discount=false because this is NOT a structural sub-par equilibrium — it is an in-band ingested price during a transient spot dislocation. Firing DEPEG here would be a wrong-source artifact (alerting on a thin-venue spot the monitor neither ingests nor should). The DEPEG must stay silent and the snapshot must raise NO WARN-or-above signal (noise ceiling) — a benign on-chain spot wick where the ingested oracle held peg must not wake the operator. Pairs with the USDe 2025-10-11 CEX-wick control (off-chain wick) as the on-chain analogue. MEASURED RE-CAPTURE: the load-bearing depeg input is now read on-chain (not hand-typed); the measured value PRESERVES the benign premise (in-band, no band/floor crossed). measured_pct=100 (peg + totalSupply both measured).
